False alarm on close inspection. The cross-project-status artifact already globs both docs/ and docs/audits/ at line ~437 of index.html (artifact updatedAt 2026-05-15T22:08:26). Path concatenation at line 442 handles both bare filenames and audits-prefixed names. No action needed.

items.prompt: HTBH Numbers tab in docs/GDD.html hand-encodes HP, cost, defense, worker counts, footprint for every v1 building. The .gd files in source/buildings/ are the actual truth. Today caught 3 rows drifted (Manor, House, Research Lab). Structural fix: add a build-time pre-processor to publish-gdd.ps1 that reads source/buildings/*.gd, extracts the @export and const values, and rewrites the Numbers tab rows. Same pattern applies to the Walls + Towers table and Units + Enemies stat tables.

YaC has two version sources, CONTEXT.md and package.json. They drift because release.ps1 only touches CONTEXT.md. Add a step to release.ps1 (or push-to-github.ps1) that reads CONTEXT.md current version pill, parses X.Y.Z, runs pnpm version <ver> --no-git-tag-version so package.json moves in lockstep. The 2026-05-15 bump to 0.26.0 was hand-applied; future bumps should be automatic.

The cross-project-status artifact (Documents/Claude/Artifacts/cross-project-status/index.html) hardcodes the previous session VM mount root at lines 380-384 and 421: /sessions/keen-gifted-wright/mnt/. Each Cowork session gets a new mount root, so the live-refresh git + queue + audit panels silently fail on every new session and the dashboard falls back to the static snapshot. Fix: discover the mount root dynamically at refresh time. Simplest: ls -d /sessions/*/mnt/YesAndEverything 2>/dev/null | head -1, then substring-extract the session segment, then build all per-repo paths from that base. Apply to all five hardcoded path references in the artifact JS.
items.source: 2026-05-15 queue-drain-frequent run, found while verifying item 014

items.prompt: GDD section 9 Tier 4 Wonders Effect column drifts from shipped code on every row and needs author judgement on which design wins. Code is the current source of truth, but the section 9 prose reads as the original design vision: Cornucopia section 9 says '+5.0 Provisions/s plus Storehouse cap halo' while code is 4.0 Provisions/s with no cap halo. Perpetual Engine section 9 says '+50 Power capacity (draw model)' while code is 5.0 Power/s production with a 60 Coal seed cost. Pyre section 9 says 'noise emitter pulling aggression, ~5000 HP, summons emergency wave on placement' while code is Brewery-pattern -6/min Terror credit and 1200 HP. The Sanctum section 9 says '-20/min Terror' while code is +1 HP/s/unit map-wide heal. Decision needed: amend section 9 to match code, or rewrite code to match section 9. Carries from 2026-05-15 second-pass audit.

GDD changelog entries jump from v0.61.8 (line 3824) to v0.61.10 (line 3823) with no v0.61.9 entry. The v0.61.10 entry frames itself as the redo of the v0.61.5 work that was lost in the v0.61.8 FUSE truncation, so v0.61.9 was likely consumed during recovery without its own changelog line. Auto-safe fix: amend the v0.61.10 opening sentence to acknowledge the skip, or insert a one-line v0.61.9 placeholder noting the skip. Either path is doc-only and reads cleanly in solo-dev voice.

Working tree carries unstaged edits across docs/GDD.html, project.godot, scripts/publish-gdd.ps1, source/autoloads/save_load.gd, source/buildings/reinforced_wall.tscn, source/enemies/enemy_base.gd, source/ui/briefing/briefing.gd, source/ui/gameplay/gameplay.gd, source/ui/hud/hud.gd, source/ui/hud/hud.tscn. Last commit is v0.61.8 while the GDD pill and project.godot config/version both read 0.61.10. Memory rule commit_per_patch_on_htbh says HARD RULE: ship every version through release.ps1 before starting the next one. git status also surfaces an unmerged path entry and a tree of binary-junk filenames at the repo root that look like FUSE write corruption. Needs manual triage on the binary-junk paths before any release.ps1 call, then ship v0.61.9 (if it exists separately) + v0.61.10 in one push. Not auto-safe.

X:\YesAndApothecary\data\aliases.json carries `"st. johns wort": "st johns wort"` which is dead weight: src/util/lookup.js strips dots before alias lookup (.replace(/\./g, '')), and PROJECT_SPEC section 4.2 explicitly states no need to alias both dot and non-dot variants. Remove the line. Auto-safe text edit. See docs/CANONICAL_AUDIT-2026-05-18.md drift #6.

X:\YesAndApothecary\PROJECT_SPEC.md section 9 roadmap carries duplicate milestone headers after M5: a second `### M3 - Export (v0.3.x)` (PNG/PDF via html2canvas/jsPDF, Avery sheet export) and a second `### M4 - Themes (v0.4.x)` (more themes + theme picker). These are leftovers from a pre-back-label roadmap revision and conflict with the real M3 (Back label) and M4 (Multi-culture data) listed earlier in the same section. Nick's call: delete entirely, demote into section 10 Backlog as parked items, or renumber into a real M6/M7 if still intended. Not auto-safe - moves milestone scope. The apothecary mirror is live at yesandeverything.com/apothecary, currently at v0.3.0, but the umbrella landing page does not present it. Add a fourth project card matching the existing pattern (article.project block with project-name, badge, project-blurb, project-meta). Suggested badge text: 'Design Tool plus Static Site plus Live'. Suggested blurb: pull a tight sentence from X:\YesAndApothecary\README.md first paragraph. Also extend the meta name=description and meta og:description tags to mention YesAndApothecary alongside the existing three.

YaC has release.ps1 + push-to-github.ps1 + discord-notify.ps1 but the publish-side integrity guard is unclear. Audit X:\YesAndChains\scripts\release.ps1 and push-to-github.ps1 for what they verify before pushing (app.js size sanity, CONTEXT.md version pill matches package.json, no conflict markers, etc). If missing, add a Test-YacIntegrity function mirroring the HTBH publish-gdd.ps1 v0.61.13 Test-GddIntegrity pattern. Checks: CONTEXT.md ends cleanly, app.js exists and > 100KB, no <<<<<<< conflict markers in tracked files, package.json version matches CONTEXT.md 'Current version' pill.

X:\YesAndApothecary\push.ps1 has commit + mirror + Discord but no file-integrity check before the deploy mirror runs. Add a Test-ApothecaryIntegrity function before the Clear-StaleGitLock $apothecaryRoot section.
